name: Notion content calendar → Posta multi-platform schedule

parameters.content:
## Notion content calendar → Posta multi-platform schedule

### What it does

Every 15 minutes, this workflow polls a Notion database for rows that are
`Status = Approved` and whose `Scheduled At` falls inside the next 15-min
window. For each row it creates a Posta post, schedules it for that time,
and updates the Notion row with the resulting Posta post URL.

### Notion database schema (required columns)

| Column                | Type           | Notes                                                                        |
|-----------------------|----------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `Caption`             | Title (text)   | The post body. Used as the Posta caption.                                    |
| `Status`              | Select         | One of: Draft, Approved, Scheduled, Posted, Failed.                          |
| `Scheduled At`        | Date (w/ time) | When the post should go live.                                                |
| `Social Account IDs`  | Text           | Comma-separated Posta social-account IDs (e.g. `35, 42, 99`).               |
| `Media IDs`           | Text (optional)| Comma-separated Posta media IDs (upload to Posta first; this is by reference)|
| `Posta Post URL`      | URL (optional) | The workflow writes the Posta post URL back here after scheduling.           |

### Setup steps

- [ ] Add your Posta credential (API token) to every Posta node
- [ ] Add your Notion integration credential to the Notion nodes
- [ ] In `Get approved rows`, set the database ID to your content calendar
- [ ] Confirm the property names match your Notion columns exactly
- [ ] Activate the workflow (top-right toggle) — the 15-min cron will start firing

### Customisation

- Tighten the schedule cadence (e.g. every 5 min) if you have many same-day rows.
- Add an optional `If — Regenerate with AI` branch after `Split In Batches` to rewrite the caption per-platform via OpenAI before posting. Out of scope for v1.

## 1. Poll Notion every 15 min

Fetches every row in the calendar whose `Status = Approved` AND `Scheduled At ≤ now + 15 min`. The cron interval and the time window are deliberately the same so each row is picked up exactly once.

## 2. Create + schedule each post

For every row returned, create a Posta post with the row's caption, social accounts and (optional) media, then schedule it for the row's `Scheduled At` time.

## 3. Write the result back to Notion

Flips the row's `Status` to `Scheduled` and writes the Posta post URL into the `Posta Post URL` column so the calendar always reflects what actually shipped.
